case_title: FUNG LEE VS. ANNIE O'KEEFFE ET AL

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS? DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 71815130) FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEE, FUNG
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-29-2024 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JAN-29-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS ANNIE O'KEEFFE, AND MICHAEL MOORE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR PER NOTICE OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ALL FUTURE LAW AND MOTION MATTERS SHOULD BE DIRECTED TO DEPARTMENT 501, WHICH HANDLES LANDLORD/TENANT CASES PER SF LOCAL RULE 2.7B. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-26-2024 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO (1) CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, THERE IS NO OTHER ACTION PENDING INVOLVING THE SAME CLAIM AND THE SAME PARTIES. THE CLAIMS FOR UNLAWFUL DETAINER AND FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT ARE NOT THE SAME; THE PLAINTIFFS, ALBERT LEE AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE AND FUNG LEE, AN INDIVIDUAL ARE NOT THE SAME. THE COURT NOTES THAT TWO SEPARATE PLAINTIFFS CLAIM OWNERSHIP IN THE SAME PROPERTY AND DO NOT ALLEGE TO BE CO-OWNERS. THIS ISSUE WAS NOT RAISED BY THE DEFENDANTS. (2) CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. DEFENDANT ABANDONED ITS ARGUMENTS HAVING NOT ADDRESSED THIS CAUSE OF ACTION IN REPLY. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, ELDER ABUSE AND UCL VIOLATION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS (NOT CONCLUSIONS) IN SUPPORT OF EACH ELEMENT OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION WITH REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CC 3345 WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO BE RE-ALLEGED WITHIN AN APPROPRIATE CAUSE OF ACTION AS RELIEF/REQUEST FOR DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-27-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Albert Lee; 415-215-6868; Appearing in person as counsel for plaintiff.
Braxton Haake and Thomas Drohan; 415-538-3333; Legal Assistance to the Elderly; Appearing in person for defendants.

Mr. Lee contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ANNIE O'KEEFFE, MICHAEL MOORE DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.
Demurrer is OVERRULED as to
(1) cause of action for breach of contract, there is no other action pending involving the same claim and the same parties. The claims for unlawful detainer and for breach of contract are not the same; the Plaintiffs, Albert Lee as successor Trustee and Fung Lee, an individual are not the same. The Court notes that two separate plaintiffs claim ownership in the same property and do not allege to be co-owners. This issue was not raised by the Defendants.
(2) Cause of action for declaratory relief. Defendant abandoned its arguments having not addressed this cause of action in reply.
Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to causes of action for fraud, elder abuse and UCL violation with leave to amend to allege facts (not conclusions) in support of each element of these causes of action with requisite specificity.
Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to cause of action for CC 3345 with leave to amend to be re-alleged within an appropriate cause of action as relief/request for damages.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-29-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS ANNIE O'KEEFFE, AND MICHAEL MOORE's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar per notice of first amended complaint. All future law and motion matters should be directed to department 501, which handles landlord/tenant cases per SF Local Rule 2.7B.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
